name: Kopiko
description: A cross-island route with substantial climbing and logistics planning.
difficultyExplanation: Kopiko is a long cross-island mission with major climbing and remote stretches. Limited refuelling windows in some sections mean strong logistics and pacing are essential.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The central Whanganui River Road is remote with limited resupply. Carry two extra days of food and confirm water before leaving small settlements.
routeSummary: Kopiko is a hard bikepacking route covering about 796 km, commonly ridden over 10 days. A cross-island route with substantial climbing and logistics planning. Riders can expect 45% Gravel / 15% Road / 40% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Oct-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The central Whanganui River Road is remote with limited resupply. Carry two extra days of food and confirm water before leaving small settlements. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 10-day route, we recommend a Hardtail MTB to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
